rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports or features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: Use explicit type declarations in AGREE to match the actual data types of AADL features.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless they are explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Write deterministic dependency guarantees that relate visible inputs to visible outputs.
Generate range assumptions using named constants or visible feature names when bounds are provided.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the actual data types of the referenced features.
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic scope in AGREE
Implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
Subcomponent port usage in component type contracts
